Content: Stapley: Yeah, that's pretty. Pretty colors.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，這很漂亮。顏色很美。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, I was looking at the end there.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，我剛剛在看最後那段。

Content: The 20th of September, October, November, December, January, February, March, April.
Content_zh: 九月二十日，十月、十一月、十二月、一月、二月、三月、四月。

Content: So yeah, everything looks cool here.
Content_zh: 所以對，這邊看起來都不錯。

Content: Yeah, those last three months right there seems like there's going to be a lot of changing.
Content_zh: 對，最後那三個月看起來會有很多變動。

Content: [Laughs} From November to February looks a little crazy.
Content_zh: [笑聲] 從十一月到二月看起來有點瘋狂。

Content: Yeah man. It's going to be pretty nuts.
Content_zh: 是啊，會很誇張。

Content: [Music] [quite fantasy music]
Content_zh: [音樂] [安靜的奇幻音樂]

Content: Petty: Well, then we need to come up with another plan because right now it's not possible.
Content_zh: Petty：那我們得想另一個計畫，因為現在這樣行不通。

Content: There's just not, one artist can't be the primary, like he's the, he's the, you know, like literally he's the renderer for every object in the game.
Content_zh: 就是不可能，一個美術不能當主要負責人，像他就是，呃，等於遊戲裡每個物件都要由他來畫完。

Content: And do concept design on the schedule we have.
Content_zh: 還要照我們的時程做概念設計。

Content: Schafer: I think if I hadn't been through the process before, I'd think the project was falling apart last month.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想如果我以前沒經歷過這個流程，我上個月大概會覺得專案快垮了。

Content: It definitely is a little bit of a tension as you switch gears and you're talking about practical matters.
Content_zh: 當你要換個方向，開始談實際問題時，確實會有一點緊張。

Content: But it wasn't that tense, you know.
Content_zh: 但其實沒有那麼緊張。

Content: Petty: For all the artists involved but certainly for Bagel, we're going to have make, you're going to have decide, I guess,
Content_zh: Petty：對所有參與的美術來說都是，但對 Bagel 特別是，我們得做出，或者說你得決定，

Content: which, what you want the hierarchy of his time to be spent on.
Content_zh: 你希望他的時間優先花在哪些事情上。

Content: [Ambient sci-fi music] Schafer: I want him to come up with both the, you know,
Content_zh: [環境科幻音樂] Schafer：我希望他能同時想出，呃，

Content: that really just like content and then actual style, both.
Content_zh: 也就是內容，還有實際的風格，兩者都要。

Content: Petty: There's other options there, that we talked about all those options.
Content_zh: Petty：還有其他選項，我們也都談過了。

Content: I don't think we've committed to one particular solution yet.
Content_zh: 我不覺得我們已經決定要採用某一個特定方案。

Content: Rice: I think it's an ongoing conversation, so not really.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得這還在持續討論，所以還沒有。

Content: Schafer: I mean yeah, we're doing, since we're doing a game that's like a different engine type than we've ever done before, it's like 2D.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，對，我們現在做的是一款引擎類型跟以前完全不同的遊戲，是 2D 的。

Content: A whole different engine, new devices we'd never worked on and an adventure game, which we would have never made a straight adventure game here at this company.
Content_zh: 完全不同的引擎、我們從沒做過的新裝置，還有冒險遊戲。老實說，我們公司本來不會做純冒險遊戲。

Content: So we're doing so many new things with this project that we're just learning.
Content_zh: 所以這個專案有太多新東西，我們都還在學。

Content: Every time we try to do something, we find out like that's not going to work.
Content_zh: 每次我們試著做一件事，就會發現那樣行不通。

Content: We do have to, of course, correct and try different solutions.
Content_zh: 我們當然得修正，再試不同的解法。

Content: But that's totally normal, expected.
Content_zh: 但這很正常，也在預期之中。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Don't fuck with my wall.
Content_zh: 畫外音：不要亂搞我的牆。

Content: Rice: But I mean, like a lot of it isn't like, there's a problem, we're fucked.
Content_zh: Rice：但我的意思是，很多狀況並不是「出問題了，我們完蛋了」。

Content: It's like we need to pay attention to this and try to start figuring out ways to make it work.
Content_zh: 比較像是「我們得注意這件事，開始想辦法讓它可行」。

Content: And we started thinking about that and feeling more comfortable.
Content_zh: 我們開始這樣思考之後，也比較安心了。

Content: Schafer: Well, we have, we brought on Levi and he's helping out.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，我們找了 Levi 加入，他正在幫忙。

Content: Rice: We have some new people, but like they're getting familiar with everything pretty quickly.
Content_zh: Rice：我們有一些新人，但他們很快就熟悉整個狀況了。

Content: Ryken: I was actually, on Friday I was looking at this.
Content_zh: Ryken：其實我週五有在看這個。

Content: Just getting inspired.
Content_zh: 只是想找點靈感。

Content: This is the museum near Tokyo.
Content_zh: 這是東京附近的博物館。

Content: It's super cool and I went there and I bought that there.
Content_zh: 超酷的，我去過那裡，這個也是在那裡買的。

Content: And for Friday, I just, the first thing in the morning I was getting inspired looking at that.
Content_zh: 然後週五一早，我就在看它找靈感。

Content: And then someone was like, you know what?
Content_zh: 接著有人就說：「你知道嗎？」

Content: They're playing a bunch of those right now and apparently for like two weeks they're playing all of those studio Ghibli films.
Content_zh: 他們現在正在放一堆那個，聽說大概兩週內會放完所有吉卜力工作室的電影。

Content: And I'm just going to be there every day.
Content_zh: 所以我每天都會去。

Content: It's happening right now. Yeah.
Content_zh: 現在就正在播。對。

Content: Over the weekend I saw three: I saw Nausicaa, which is like a big inspiration for stuff that we're doing right now.
Content_zh: 週末我看了三部：我看了《風之谷》，那對我們現在做的東西是很大的靈感來源。

Content: [Offscreen] Yeah we just watched that last week.
Content_zh: [畫外音] 對，我們上週才看過那部。

Content: Really great, it's really great.
Content_zh: 真的很棒，真的很棒。

Content: Rice: Yeah. We had like another art slot.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我們還有一個美術名額。

Content: We didn't know what we were going to fill it with and like right now it makes sense
Content_zh: 我們本來不知道要找誰來補，但現在看起來很合理，

Content: because concepts the most needed and that's why Levi's around.
Content_zh: 因為目前最需要的是概念設計，所以 Levi 才會在這裡。

Content: But I mean, who knows?
Content_zh: 不過誰知道呢？

Content: Like later in the project we could be ramping down on concept and need more animation and have to switch it out.
Content_zh: 專案後期也許概念需求會降低，反而需要更多動畫，到時候就得調整。

Content: But yeah, we always had one other spot for an artist and I'm glad that it was Levi.
Content_zh: 不過對，我們一直都有另一個美術職缺，我很高興最後是 Levi。

Content: Offscreen Voice: How far along did Peter Chan get with his contributions?
Content_zh: 畫外音：Peter Chan 的貢獻大概做到什麼程度？

Content: Rice: How far did Peter get?
Content_zh: Rice：Peter 做到哪裡？

Content: I think the last stuff that Peter did was some environment designs for spaceship stuff.
Content_zh: 我想 Peter 最後做的是一些太空船相關的環境設計。

Content: Schafer: All right. It's been good talking to you, Peter.
Content_zh: Schafer：好吧。Peter，跟你合作很愉快。

Content: Chan: Thank you again, you guys, for everything and it was a lot of fun.
Content_zh: Chan：也再次謝謝你們的一切，這真的很好玩。

Content: And to be part of it.
Content_zh: 能參與其中也很棒。

Content: So thank you for inviting me.
Content_zh: 所以謝謝你們邀請我。

Content: Schafer: We'll see you again. Petty: Thanks, Peter.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們會再見的。Petty：謝了，Peter。

Content: Rice: Yeah, it was a bit of a bummer.
Content_zh: Rice：對，這有點可惜。

Content: I mean, he was offsite so it wasn't like we were hanging out all the time, which would have been super fun.
Content_zh: 我是說，他是遠端工作，所以我們不是一直能一起混在一起，不然一定會超好玩。

Content: But still, like I did look forward to every Friday, getting like a drop of art from him.
Content_zh: 但我還是很期待每個星期五都能收到他交來的一批美術圖。

Content: But Levi's around so like having him right next door and getting to see his stuff, which has been like a totally different take on it and you know,
Content_zh: 不過 Levi 就在這裡，讓他坐在隔壁，能看到他的東西，而且那又是完全不同的詮釋，

Content: being able to hang out with him in the office has been fun.
Content_zh: 能在辦公室跟他一起相處也很有趣。

Content: Rice: Yeah. I don't know if you talked to Lee much about what you're going to start with, concept-wise.
Content_zh: Rice：對。我不知道你有沒有跟 Lee 多聊過概念上你要先從哪裡開始。

Content: Ryken: Yeah, I have concept stuff for boy world stuff.
Content_zh: Ryken：有，我有一些男孩世界的概念東西。

Content: Rice: Did you check out Peter's? Ryken: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：你有看過 Peter 的嗎？Ryken：有。

Content: Rice: Yeah, are you scared?
Content_zh: Rice：對，那你怕了嗎？

Content: It's a lot to live up to, man.
Content_zh: 這壓力很大啊。

Content: Ryken: I'll get out my tracing paper.
Content_zh: Ryken：我會把描圖紙拿出來。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I don't think it works that way.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我覺得那樣行不通。

Content: Ryken: Peter Chan did a bunch of the early stuff and I've been looking at what he's been doing.
Content_zh: Ryken：Peter Chan 做了很多早期的東西，我一直在看他做的內容。

Content: This is me, actually look at this.
Content_zh: 這是我，真的，你看這個。

Content: That's bootleg P. Chan.
Content_zh: 那是盜版 P. Chan。

Content: Schafer: And we have interviewed an intern, an art intern.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們也面試了一位實習生，美術實習生。

Content: I haven't met her yet, but everyone seemed to like her and think she was good.
Content_zh: 我還沒見過她，但大家好像都很喜歡她，也覺得她不錯。

Content: And that would help, too.
Content_zh: 那也會有幫助。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Oh no, you filmed that!
Content_zh: 畫外音：糟了，你拍下來了！

Content: Schafer: Please also join me in welcoming Marius from Germany, where did he go...
Content_zh: Schafer：也請大家一起歡迎來自德國的 Marius，他去哪了……

Content: There he is! Marius Fitz...
Content_zh: 在那裡！Marius Fitz……

Content: I'm going to have you pronounce your last name.
Content_zh: 我要請你自己念你的姓。

Content: Can you pronounce your last name for us?
Content_zh: 可以幫我們念一下你的姓嗎？

Content: Fietzek: Fietzek.
Content_zh: Fietzek：Fietzek。

Content: Schafer: You know, Marius was the one who made an adventure game as a job application for Double Fine, which seems very nice.
Content_zh: Schafer：你們知道，Marius 是那個做了一款冒險遊戲當作 Double Fine 求職作品的人，這真的很棒。

Content: Fietzek: Hey, I heard about the Double Fine Adventure and was super-hyped and yeah, I thought like if I want to,
Content_zh: Fietzek：嘿，我聽說《Double Fine Adventure!》之後超興奮，然後我想，如果我想，

Content: if there's a chance to work with my idols on the 2D animated game, then this is like the only chance maybe ever.
Content_zh: 如果有機會跟我的偶像一起做 2D 動畫遊戲，那可能就是這輩子唯一的機會了。

Content: So I made an, application video game.
Content_zh: 所以我做了一款求職用的電玩遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Our plan is to actually release that as a Double Fine Adventure and then pocket all the Kickstarter money.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們的計畫是把它真的當成《Double Fine Adventure!》發行，然後把所有 Kickstarter 的錢都收進口袋。

Content: We'll give you a small percentage of it.
Content_zh: 我們會分你一小部分。

Content: How long will Marius be here, Mr. Greg Rice?
Content_zh: Greg Rice 先生，Marius 會待多久？

Content: Rice: Three months.
Content_zh: Rice：三個月。

Content: Schafer: You get three months, holy cow.
Content_zh: Schafer：你有三個月，天啊。

Content: And what will he be doing?
Content_zh: 那他會做什麼？

Content: Rice: Who knows? Schafer: Who knows.
Content_zh: Rice：誰知道？Schafer：誰知道。

Content: Rice: Animating. Schafer: You'll do some animating on Red's.
Content_zh: Rice：動畫。Schafer：你會幫 Red’s 做一些動畫。

Content: Fietzek: But I started with the whole cinematic approach on the very first cut scene, just to see how it feels.
Content_zh: Fietzek：但我一開始是用很電影化的方式處理第一個過場動畫，只是想看看感覺如何。

Content: I thought of a very quiet start where we first see the spaceship, because I don't, I don't remember a point at the space boy part
Content_zh: 我想像一個很安靜的開場，讓我們先看到太空船，因為我不記得太空男孩那部分有哪個時刻，

Content: where we actually see the ship.
Content_zh: 是真的看到那艘船的。

Content: Schafer: Well, Marius is great.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，Marius 很棒。

Content: Like he's like, he'll just show us, you know, a story board and it's really hard to like how someone do something
Content_zh: 他會直接拿故事板給我們看。要跟一個人說「你做的東西真的很棒、很好笑，但也許不太符合我們想法」其實很難。

Content: that's really great and really funny, but just for what you were thinking of it, maybe it's not right.
Content_zh: 那真的很棒、很好笑，但就你原本想的方向來說，也許不對。

Content: Schafer: And the same way the girl starts.
Content_zh: Schafer：女孩的開場也是一樣。

Content: She's just laying on the hillside and it's not a cut scene, but like a steady state; you exit out of it by tapping.
Content_zh: 她只是躺在山坡上，那不是過場動畫，而是一個靜止的互動畫面；點一下就會離開。

Content: And the same way with this.
Content_zh: 這邊也一樣。

Content: It's really got to be that it pans over and shows him and he's laying there on the bed, and the same kind of just, you know.
Content_zh: 真的應該是鏡頭平移過去，看到他躺在床上，然後也是那種感覺。

Content: [Schafer] So you got to like tell him, like I had to tell him like this whole sequence was backwards.
Content_zh: [Schafer] 所以你得跟他說，就像我得跟他說，這整段順序是反的。

Content: And he's like, oh great, OK, great.
Content_zh: 他就說：「喔太好了，OK，很好。」

Content: And then I come back the next day and it's completely different.
Content_zh: 然後我隔天回來，它就完全不一樣了。

Content: Exactly the way I wanted and it's perfect and it's like, oh, that's great.
Content_zh: 完全就是我想要的樣子，很完美，我就覺得：「喔，太棒了。」

Content: [Schafer vocalizing sound effects] Schafer: That's awesome.
Content_zh: [Schafer 模仿音效] Schafer：太棒了。

Content: Schafer: You know, it's like, finding someone like that is really, it's hard to find people like that and so you kind of hang onto them.
Content_zh: Schafer：你知道，要找到這樣的人真的很難，所以你會想把他留住。

Content: Of course, he's supposed to go back to school.
Content_zh: 當然，他本來應該回去上學。

Content: So then you have to figure out whether you want to derail someone's education by trying to recruit them to come to your company.
Content_zh: 所以你接著就得想清楚，要不要為了招募他來公司而打亂別人的學業。

Content: Which of course, I have no problem with.
Content_zh: 當然，我完全不介意。

Content: I'm already done thinking about it and I've already thought about it while I was saying that sentence, so, so much for his education.
Content_zh: 我已經想完了，而且我在說那句話的時候就已經想完了，所以他的學業就先這樣吧。

Content: [Laughter] Rice: We're finally like fully staffed and we've got like our team,
Content_zh: [笑聲] Rice：我們終於算是人手到齊了，也有自己的團隊了，

Content: all of our budget's totally spent on everyone who's on the team.
Content_zh: 我們全部預算都花在團隊成員身上了。

Content: So I think that, you know, a month or so from now it's going to feel like everything's just kind of moving forward steadily and the game is being made.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得，大概一個月後，一切應該會開始穩定往前，遊戲也會真的被做出來。

Content: Rice: At the time when we started the Maiden Feast, that was kind of the only puzzle that was far enough along
Content_zh: Rice：當時我們開始做 Maiden Feast 時，那算是唯一進度夠前面的謎題，

Content: that we had enough of a design to actually start building stuff.
Content_zh: 我們有足夠的設計內容，可以真的開始製作東西。

Content: So we did know going in that it was like a super kind of meaty puzzle that was going to be a tough one to implement.
Content_zh: 所以我們一開始就知道，那是個很有份量的謎題，實作起來會很硬。

Content: And we still, you know, it was kind of all we had to go on at the point.
Content_zh: 而且那時候我們也只有那個可以依據。

Content: Schafer: I had the Maiden's Feast designed so they had to like, oh, we got to pick that, 'cause that's the only thing that's really designed.
Content_zh: Schafer：我已經把 Maiden’s Feast 設計好了，所以他們就得選那個，因為那是唯一真正設計好的東西。

Content: Rice: But by the time we'd gotten it to like the first pass kind of state, then we had designs for this Cloud Colony stuff,
Content_zh: Rice：但等我們把它做到第一版狀態時，我們也已經有 Cloud Colony 這些內容的設計，

Content: which we knew was just a lot easier to implement and a lot more indicative of the rest of the game.
Content_zh: 而我們知道那個實作起來容易很多，也更能代表遊戲其他部分。

Content: Schafer: So navigation across different layers and pointing and clicking, talking to people, camera motion is all part of Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以跨不同層級移動、點擊、跟人說話、鏡頭運動，都是 Cloud Colony 的一部分。

Content: Rice: One of the nice things about being able to make those kinds of decisions and jump around is we can like really look at in the moment
Content_zh: Rice：能做這種決定、能在不同部分之間跳來跳去的好處之一，是我們可以真的看當下，

Content: what is most important for the game and be able to kind of like change tracks, without having to like clear that decision with a publisher.
Content_zh: 什麼對遊戲最重要，然後轉換方向，不必先拿去給發行商批准。

Content: Schafer: So we kind of left Maiden Feast where it was, which is, you know, has like a stand-in, a couple stand-in pieces of art in there.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們把 Maiden Feast 先停在那裡，裡面有一些暫用的美術素材。

Content: Much as it pains me to leave that unfinished, but we'll revisit it soon, I think.
Content_zh: 雖然把它留在未完成狀態讓我很痛苦，但我想我們很快會回頭處理。

Content: Schafer: This is a kick-off meeting for the Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是 Cloud Colony 的啟動會議。

Content: Required reading for this meeting is this document everyone' read, I'm sure.
Content_zh: 這次會議的必讀資料是這份文件，我相信大家都看過了。

Content: I've already started working on it.
Content_zh: 我已經開始做了。

Content: So I think it's just good to go over all the puzzles in case it exposes things we haven't thought of or just questions you guys have.
Content_zh: 所以我想把所有謎題都過一遍是好事，看看會不會暴露出我們沒想到的事，或你們有什麼問題。

Content: Are you ready? Ready to begin?
Content_zh: 準備好了嗎？準備開始了嗎？

Content: Schafer: This is the part of the game right after the Maiden's Feast when you get, when you break away and you hitch a ride on that buzzard, on the corset.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是遊戲裡 Maiden’s Feast 之後的部分。你逃出來，搭上那隻禿鷹，掛在束腹上。

Content: And you fly.
Content_zh: 然後你飛起來。

Content: It takes you up into the air.
Content_zh: 牠會把你帶到空中。

Content: She's the sacrifice girl who's just chosen to run away instead of being sacrificed and feels that she may have just endangered,
Content_zh: 她是那個獻祭女孩，她選擇逃跑，而不是被獻祭。她覺得自己可能害了大家，

Content: she's been told that this will endanger her entire town if the monster's not appeased.
Content_zh: 因為她被告知，如果沒有安撫怪物，整個村鎮都會有危險。

Content: So now the monster's all upset and she's feeling like I have to do something to get rid of this monster and to protect my family and my town.
Content_zh: 所以現在怪物很生氣，她覺得自己必須做點什麼，擺脫這個怪物，保護家人和村鎮。

Content: But before I get back there I'm going to have to like figure out a plan.
Content_zh: 但在她回去之前，她得先想出一個計畫。

Content: So she arrives in the home world, if you will, of the bird, which is a little village made of primarily nests that have been,
Content_zh: 所以她來到了那隻鳥的「家鄉」，可以這麼說，是一個主要由鳥巢構成的小村莊，

Content: that are buoyant on these really thick clouds, that we're calling the Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: 那些鳥巢漂浮在很厚的雲上，我們稱它為 Cloud Colony。

Content: [Music] Schafer: I feel much better because I actually, like I felt good
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我感覺好多了，因為其實，

Content: after I wrote that cloud document, but that was really only like four puzzles.
Content_zh: 我寫完那份雲端殖民地設計文件後感覺不錯，但那其實只有大概四個謎題。

Content: And the spaceship one has like sixteen puzzles in there or something.
Content_zh: 而太空船那邊有十六個左右的謎題。

Content: So I'm catching up now.
Content_zh: 所以我現在正在趕上進度。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Aha! Work. I did work.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：啊哈！工作。我有工作。

Content: This is the spec for the whole spaceship.
Content_zh: 這是整艘太空船的規格文件。

Content: Designed all the puzzles for the spaceship.
Content_zh: 太空船的所有謎題都設計好了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Really? All the puzzles?
Content_zh: 畫外音：真的？所有謎題？

Content: Schafer: Yeah, all the puzzles for the spaceship.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，太空船的所有謎題。

Content: And this is just two-thirds of the puzzles for the girl's world.
Content_zh: 而這只是女孩世界三分之二的謎題。

Content: But there's still, I mean, there's still a lot of stuff to design.
Content_zh: 不過還有，我是說，還有很多東西要設計。

Content: Schafer: But it's like I have plot points, you know.
Content_zh: Schafer：但我有劇情點，你知道。

Content: I have things where I know I want that to end, Act One.
Content_zh: 我知道有些事情要在第一幕結束。

Content: And then that ends Act Two.
Content_zh: 然後那邊會結束第二幕。

Content: And then I have these plot points that happen.
Content_zh: 然後我有這些會發生的劇情點。

Content: And then to get to that plot point you have to create obstacles and puzzles at the same time, which come out of the story.
Content_zh: 接著為了抵達那些劇情點，你必須同時創造障礙和謎題，而它們要從故事裡長出來。

Content: Break out of routine.
Content_zh: 打破日常。

Content: Spaceship boy has this routine that he's stuck in every day in space and he has to break out of it.
Content_zh: 太空船男孩每天在太空裡被困在同一套日常中，他得打破它。

Content: That's the first puzzle.
Content_zh: 那就是第一個謎題。

Content: You do work backwards.
Content_zh: 你是倒著做的。

Content: Like you know, it's like you actually aren't designing puzzles.
Content_zh: 就像，其實你不是在設計謎題。

Content: You're designing the obstacles in a lot of ways.
Content_zh: 很多時候你是在設計障礙。

Content: Like you say, you had a situation in the game where character, you just know there's a plot point where they have to shoot the President.
Content_zh: 比方說，遊戲裡有個情境，你知道有個劇情點是角色必須射殺總統。

Content: And so, I don't have any plans to shoot the President.
Content_zh: 先說，我沒有任何射殺總統的計畫。

Content: So, but anyways, say that was a puzzle in the game.
Content_zh: 但總之，假設那是遊戲裡的一個謎題。

Content: The President's really bad.
Content_zh: 總統真的很壞。

Content: You got to shoot the President.
Content_zh: 你得射殺總統。

Content: You know, I work backwards, so some cool wish fulfillment, like OK, he's going to be, he's sneaking into the White House.
Content_zh: 你知道，我會倒著推，所以那是某種很酷的願望實現。好，他要偷偷溜進白宮。

Content: How would you sneak into White House?
Content_zh: 你要怎麼溜進白宮？

Content: You could wear a mask that made you look like a visiting ambassador.
Content_zh: 你可以戴一個面具，讓自己看起來像來訪的大使。

Content: Like how do you make a plaster cast of his face to make the mask?
Content_zh: 那你要怎麼用他的臉做石膏模型來做面具？

Content: Then maybe it's to trick him into tripping and falling in cement.
Content_zh: 也許方法是騙他絆倒，臉摔進水泥裡。

Content: And then at each point you think, OK, was that an actual revelation?
Content_zh: 然後每一步你都會想，OK，這有帶來真正的發現嗎？

Content: Like you don't want to have, it just be a series of tedious chores.
Content_zh: 你不希望它只是一連串乏味的雜事。

Content: It's got to be like oh, I have a really clear problem that I need to solve.
Content_zh: 它必須是「喔，我有一個很明確、需要解決的問題」。

Content: Like the three most obvious ways to solve it don't work.
Content_zh: 然後三個最直覺的解法都行不通。

Content: And the game anticipates that and like has a great response for when you try to execute that solution.
Content_zh: 遊戲要預判玩家會那樣做，並在玩家嘗試那個解法時給出很棒的回應。

Content: You put a little like, aha!
Content_zh: 你放一點像是：「啊哈！」

Content: Good try, no.
Content_zh: 「不錯，但不行。」

Content: But did you think about this?
Content_zh: 「不過你有想過這個嗎？」

Content: And that little nudge is like, oh, you know, kind of, like doesn't give you the answer
Content_zh: 那個小提示就像：「喔，你知道，」它不會直接把答案給你，

Content: but kind of points you back towards the ballpark where the answer is.
Content_zh: 但會把你指回答案所在的大致範圍。

Content: Schafer: This is the first puzzle.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是第一個謎題。

Content: So the puzzle is that you open this a little bit.
Content_zh: 謎題是你把這個打開一點。

Content: And then you try to go to it, it closes.
Content_zh: 然後你試著過去，它就關起來。

Content: And then there's some really counterintuitive, almost opposite of normal way of thinking that makes you realize, ah!
Content_zh: 接著會有某個非常違反直覺、幾乎跟正常思考相反的東西，讓你意識到：「啊！」

Content: If I did this really weird thing, that would actually work.
Content_zh: 如果我做這件很奇怪的事，反而真的會成功。

Content: The thing you had to do with the lock is take the lock and actually lock the gate shut.
Content_zh: 你要對那把鎖做的事，是拿鎖把門鎖死。

Content: So now when you try to pull the chain, it can't pull the gate up and instead...
Content_zh: 所以現在你拉鍊子的時候，它拉不起門，反而……

Content: 'Cause then the player's like, oh, I'm so smart, you know.
Content_zh: 因為這樣玩家就會覺得：「喔，我真聰明。」

Content: I figured it out.
Content_zh: 我解出來了。

Content: Now that a lot of stuff is designed ahead of those guys and they're able to look at it and work on it.
Content_zh: 現在很多東西都已經先設計好，他們可以看著那些內容開始做。

Content: That always relieves a little bit of tension.
Content_zh: 這總是能稍微減輕一點壓力。

Content: Rice: Tim has filled in, it looks like boy world and I think he's even put more girl world stuff in.
Content_zh: Rice：Tim 已經補上了，看起來像是男孩世界，我想他甚至又放了更多女孩世界的東西進去。

Content: Have you even looked at this, really, Levi?
Content_zh: Levi，你真的看過這個嗎？

Content: Ryken: This is all Tim does?
Content_zh: Ryken：這全都是 Tim 做的？

Content: I thought he was supposed to be writing things, not just writing little things on Post-its.
Content_zh: 我以為他應該要寫東西，不只是把小東西寫在便利貼上。

Content: Rice: Yeah, he's writing the whole game on little Post-its.
Content_zh: Rice：對，他正在用小便利貼寫整個遊戲。

Content: Ryken: Oh, OK.
Content_zh: Ryken：喔，OK。

Content: Rice: There wasn't much there before, so it's nice to see that it's filled out.
Content_zh: Rice：之前那裡沒什麼內容，所以看到它被補起來感覺很好。

Content: Rice: It's definitely unique and different than anything he's designed or written before.
Content_zh: Rice：這絕對很獨特，跟他以前設計或寫過的任何東西都不一樣。

Content: So I think it's cool to see that he's trying something different and kind of going out on a limb a little bit.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得看到他嘗試不同的東西、稍微冒點險，滿酷的。

Content: I haven't seen any writing yet, so I don't know what that's going to be like.
Content_zh: 我還沒看到任何劇本，所以不知道會是什麼樣子。

Content: Because it does seem like this game has a much more serious tone than his other games have,
Content_zh: 因為這款遊戲的調性確實看起來比他的其他遊戲嚴肅很多，

Content: but I'm excited to get to that point where I start seeing Tim's writing, because I'm sure it'll be awesome.
Content_zh: 但我很期待看到 Tim 寫出來的內容，因為我相信一定會很棒。

Content: Rice: So far this is in the game.
Content_zh: Rice：目前這些已經在遊戲裡了。

Content: This is all in the game.
Content_zh: 這些都在遊戲裡。

Content: It looks like this.
Content_zh: 看起來是這樣。

Content: This is all in the game.
Content_zh: 這些都在遊戲裡。

Content: And now we're going to be starting on some of this stuff.
Content_zh: 然後我們現在要開始做這些東西。

Content: Rice: And we have an approved girl, so that's exciting.
Content_zh: Rice：而且我們的女孩已經核准了，所以很令人興奮。

Content: Petty: So let's see.
Content_zh: Petty：那我們來看看。

Content: So let's look at the suicide girl. So she's got...
Content_zh: 我們來看這個自殺女孩。她有……

Content: Schafer: See, you call her suicide girl, too.
Content_zh: Schafer：你看，你也叫她自殺女孩。

Content: Petty: Yeah. Schafer: It's sacrifice girl.
Content_zh: Petty：對。Schafer：她是獻祭女孩。

Content: Petty: I know it is.
Content_zh: Petty：我知道。

Content: So which of these dresses do you like the most?
Content_zh: 所以這幾件洋裝你最喜歡哪一件？

Content: Schafer: My favorite one is that one, but I like the original one.
Content_zh: Schafer：我最喜歡那件，但我也喜歡原本那件。

Content: I like that one, too.
Content_zh: 我也喜歡那件。

Content: Schafer: So I just put them in front of my daughter and let her pick.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我就把它們放到我女兒面前，讓她選。

Content: That solved the whole problem.
Content_zh: 這就解決整個問題了。

Content: Schafer: These are the princesses from papa's new game.
Content_zh: Schafer：這些是爸爸新遊戲裡的公主。

Content: Schafer: I call them princesses.
Content_zh: Schafer：我叫她們公主。

Content: Which one do you like best?
Content_zh: 你最喜歡哪一個？

Content: Think really hard.
Content_zh: 認真想喔。

Content: Why do you like em?
Content_zh: 你為什麼喜歡她？

Content: Daughter: Because she's so beautiful.
Content_zh: 女兒：因為她好漂亮。

Content: Rice: And she's looking good.
Content_zh: Rice：她看起來不錯。

Content: We just got her in the game.
Content_zh: 我們剛把她放進遊戲裡。

Content: She's walking around and everything.
Content_zh: 她已經會走來走去了。

Content: The boy's getting there.
Content_zh: 男孩也快好了。

Content: We've done like two rounds on him.
Content_zh: 我們已經做了兩輪。

Content: Schafer: Scott's been working on characters in the morning.
Content_zh: Schafer：Scott 早上一直在做角色。

Content: He's been kind of splitting his time with a book that he's working on, an art show or one of the zillion things that he does.
Content_zh: 他一直把時間分給他正在做的書、藝術展，或他做的無數事情之一。

Content: He's very busy.
Content_zh: 他非常忙。

Content: Campbell: So it's an ongoing kind of, kind of project and this is the culmination of it.
Content_zh: Campbell：所以這是一個持續進行的專案，而這是它的成果。

Content: Culmination of like a year, you know, like a year and a half's worth right now.
Content_zh: 算是一年，大概一年半累積到現在的成果。

Content: And then we have a book coming out, so that's what we're doing, so.
Content_zh: 然後我們還有一本書要出版，所以這就是我們在做的事。

Content: Schafer: He's drawn some of the sacrifice girl and spaceship boy.
Content_zh: Schafer：他畫了一些獻祭女孩和太空船男孩。

Content: He's been drawing some of the Cloud Colony people.
Content_zh: 他也畫了一些 Cloud Colony 的居民。

Content: And I'm supposed to look at those, right.
Content_zh: 而我應該要去看那些，對吧。

Content: I've got to give him feedback on those.
Content_zh: 我得給他那些圖的回饋。

Content: Schafer: I do like that guy, too.
Content_zh: Schafer：我也喜歡那個傢伙。

Content: But then again he is doing a cool pose.
Content_zh: 不過話說回來，他的姿勢確實很酷。

Content: Campbell: And it's going great, man.
Content_zh: Campbell：一切進展得很順利，老兄。

Content: It's looking really good.
Content_zh: 看起來真的很不錯。

Content: I definitely feel that like relaxation of like knowing that like these designs, even if they're a little unfinished,
Content_zh: 我確實有種放鬆的感覺，知道這些設計就算還有點未完成，

Content: I know they're going to go to Bagel and he's going to make them like, fucking make 'em Bagel-style.
Content_zh: 我知道它們會交到 Bagel 手上，他會把它們做成那種，該死的 Bagel 風格。

Content: Dude, I just swore again.
Content_zh: 老兄，我又罵髒話了。

Content: I mean, I thought for sure I wasn't going to swear in these things.
Content_zh: 我是說，我本來很確定自己不會在這些訪談裡罵髒話。

Content: I'm mellowing out, dude.
Content_zh: 老兄，我變溫和了。

Content: But yeah, he's doing great.
Content_zh: 不過對，他做得很好。

Content: He has a lot of work ahead of him, though.
Content_zh: 但他接下來還有很多工作。

Content: He's stressed about it, but Bagel's the kind of guy, he's like just this kind of chill dude, you know.
Content_zh: 他對這件事有壓力，但 Bagel 是那種人，他就是很 chill 的傢伙，你知道。

Content: He's like, I know.
Content_zh: 他會說：「我知道。」

Content: He's like, I'm going to, he's like, I'm going to have a lot of work ahead of me, but ah.
Content_zh: 他會說：「我接下來會有很多工作要做，不過，啊。」

Content: 'Cause it's like it's all fun stuff, you know, he's doing what he loves, which is like painting that stuff, you know.
Content_zh: 因為那些都是有趣的事，你知道，他在做自己喜歡的事，也就是畫那些東西。

Content: And he's definitely, he's definitely feeling kind of nervous, but I'm so excited for it to be his, his look, so yeah.
Content_zh: 他確實有點緊張，但我非常期待最後呈現出他的風格。對。

Content: Offscreen Voice: So Bagel's in the studio this week? Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以 Bagel 這週在工作室？Rice：對。

Content: Bagel's in my house this week. Offscreen Voice: Perfect.
Content_zh: Bagel 這週住我家。畫外音：完美。

Content: Rice: He had to take Marius's spot from the couch.
Content_zh: Rice：他只好從沙發上佔走 Marius 的位置。

Content: So Marius got kicked out.
Content_zh: 所以 Marius 被踢出去了。

Content: I'm like running a hostel right now.
Content_zh: 我現在簡直像在經營青年旅館。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, it's weird.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，很怪。

Content: There's a lot of farting going on.
Content_zh: 到處都有人放屁。

Content: He has these weird nerdy glasses that he wears at night.
Content_zh: 他晚上會戴那種很怪的宅宅眼鏡。

Content: Schafer: Why is Bagel in the office this week?
Content_zh: Schafer：Bagel 這週為什麼在辦公室？

Content: I don't know.
Content_zh: 我不知道。

Content: Hi guys.
Content_zh: 嗨，各位。

Content: Schafer: Hi, oh my god you look so real.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗨，天啊，你看起來好真實。

Content: Lee: He's come out of the camera!
Content_zh: Lee：他從攝影機裡出來了！

Content: Schafer: The microphone is working so well today.
Content_zh: Schafer：今天麥克風效果真好。

Content: Bagel: My microphone is awesome!
Content_zh: Bagel：我的麥克風超棒！

Content: Crook: Now we can't turn you down.
Content_zh: Crook：現在我們沒辦法把你音量調低了。

Content: Schafer: Lee was starting some new processes with art production, like trying to get Bagel to be pretty independent
Content_zh: Schafer：Lee 開始為美術製作建立一些新的流程，像是讓 Bagel 能更獨立，

Content: where he could just paint actual art for the game right into the engines.
Content_zh: 讓他可以直接把遊戲真正要用的美術畫進引擎裡。

Content: So getting that set up was really hard to do over Skype or Google Hang Outs, like we do.
Content_zh: 所以要把那套流程架起來，透過 Skype 或 Google Hangouts 真的很難，雖然我們就是這樣做。

Content: We don't always have the smoothest conversations over that, I don't know if you noticed.
Content_zh: 我們透過那些工具對話時不總是很順，不知道你有沒有注意到。

Content: Stapley: [inaudible] Petty: I think we're going to do it tomorrow.
Content_zh: Stapley：[聽不清楚] Petty：我想我們明天會做。

Content: Stapley: [inaudible] Petty: What's that?
Content_zh: Stapley：[聽不清楚] Petty：什麼？

Content: Stapley: [inaudible] Petty: I'm sorry, I can't make it out, dude.
Content_zh: Stapley：[聽不清楚] Petty：抱歉，老兄，我聽不出來。

Content: Stapley: [inaudible] [Laughing]
Content_zh: Stapley：[聽不清楚] [笑聲]

Content: Stapley: But it is good to kind of sit right with them, you know.
Content_zh: Stapley：但能跟他們坐在一起其實滿好的，你知道。

Content: Crook: Yeah, I think this texture, the painting style goes really well with this sort of weird... Stapley: Brush stroke.
Content_zh: Crook：對，我覺得這個材質、這種繪畫風格，跟這種奇怪的……Stapley：筆觸。

Content: Crook: the way we're animating it.
Content_zh: Crook：跟我們做動畫的方式很搭。

Content: I think it goes really well with it.
Content_zh: 我覺得真的很搭。

Content: I think it's cool.
Content_zh: 我覺得很酷。

Content: Stapley: But yeah, there are some things that are easier just to talk to, like walk over to Lee and talk to him, I think.
Content_zh: Stapley：不過對，有些事情就是當面講比較容易，像是直接走去找 Lee 說。

Content: I've already done that like three times today, which is pretty nice.
Content_zh: 我今天已經這樣做三次了，感覺滿好的。

Content: Rice: And here like with such a small art team, like it's going to be really important that he's not only painting stuff
Content_zh: Rice：而且在這麼小的美術團隊裡，很重要的是他不只要畫東西，

Content: but like making sure it gets into the game right and looks good and he's viewing it in the game and seeing how that changes.
Content_zh: 還要確保東西正確放進遊戲、看起來好，而且他能在遊戲裡看它，看到它會怎麼變化。

Content: Petty: So like Brandon spent a lot of time making sure that all the tools he was writing
Content_zh: Petty：所以 Brandon 花了很多時間確保他寫的所有工具，

Content: were going to help Bagel and make sure that that was a really smooth process.
Content_zh: 都能幫到 Bagel，並確保整個流程非常順。

Content: Brandon: The main thing that I've been working is enhancements to our scene editor so that the tool works a little bit more like Photoshop
Content_zh: Brandon：我主要在做的是強化我們的場景編輯器，讓這個工具用起來更像 Photoshop，

Content: in terms of being able to hide and show the layers that you're working on.
Content_zh: 也就是能隱藏或顯示你正在處理的圖層。

Content: I got the Nav meshes in there so that instead of just editing these in text files you can actually,
Content_zh: 我也把 Nav mesh 放進去了，這樣你不必只在文字檔裡編輯這些東西，

Content: you know, individually kind of lay out the geometry for the walkable areas.
Content_zh: 你可以個別配置可行走區域的幾何形狀。

Content: Just really trying to flesh the stuff out so that the workflow for artists is a lot smoother.
Content_zh: 就是盡量把這些東西補完整，讓美術的工作流程順很多。

Content: Rice: And it's almost there so I think that's the, that's the idea, is to try to get him comfortable with that process.
Content_zh: Rice：已經快完成了，所以我想重點就是讓他熟悉這個流程。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, so you can see...
Content_zh: Stapley：對，所以你可以看到……

Content: Right. Right, right.
Content_zh: 對。對，對。

Content: Still totally getting used to this shit.
Content_zh: 我還在完全適應這玩意兒。

Content: [Music] Stapley: Hey, bros. What's up?
Content_zh: [音樂] Stapley：嘿，兄弟們。怎樣？

Content: How's it going, fellows?
Content_zh: 各位最近如何？

Content: Offscreen Voice: So, has this been coming along?
Content_zh: 畫外音：所以，這個進展如何？

Content: Stapley: Oh, it's almost done, it's almost done.
Content_zh: Stapley：喔，快完成了，快完成了。

Content: No, I'm just kidding.
Content_zh: 沒有啦，我開玩笑的。

Content: It's not done at all.
Content_zh: 根本還沒完成。

Content: I'm going to give up on it and come back to it later.
Content_zh: 我打算先放棄它，晚點再回來做。

Content: And there's a bunch of other shit to do and I like don't have any fresh eyes on it anymore, you know.
Content_zh: 而且還有一堆其他鳥事要做，我已經沒有新鮮眼光可以看它了，你知道。

Content: I've just been looking at the same thing for like a week and I'm over it.
Content_zh: 我已經看同一個東西看了一個禮拜，膩了。

Content: I'm going to just work on it until stand-ups and then Tim's going to check it out.
Content_zh: 我會一直做到站會，然後 Tim 會來看。

Content: Petty: In terms of like how good the readability is.
Content_zh: Petty：就可讀性有多好來說。

Content: If it's feeling right to you, you know, story, theme-wise and all that, before he digs into some of the other areas.
Content_zh: 在他深入其他區域之前，要看你覺得感覺對不對，故事、主題之類的。

Content: And then you know, like there's a lot of, like because that Cloud Colony...
Content_zh: 然後你知道，因為 Cloud Colony 有很多……

Content: Schafer: I can see how the world's going to look pretty and how it's going to all look like it's one cohesive thing,
Content_zh: Schafer：我看得出這個世界會很漂亮，也會看起來像一個有凝聚力的整體，

Content: but the secret thing that I'm hoping for is still, we have some of the, Bagel's ideas that only he would come up with for the landscape.
Content_zh: 但我暗自希望的還是，我們能保留一些 Bagel 對地景的想法，那些只有他才會想到的東西。

Content: Kind of bizarre.
Content_zh: 有點怪誕。

Content: He doesn't have those in there yet.
Content_zh: 他還沒把那些放進去。

Content: Stapley: Yeah, this was Peter's, Peter's concept ideas.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，這是 Peter 的，Peter 的概念想法。

Content: Lee's layout.
Content_zh: Lee 的配置。

Content: Would I do a painting of this?
Content_zh: 我會畫這個嗎？

Content: I'm not sure.
Content_zh: 我不確定。

Content: I don't know if I would.
Content_zh: 我不知道自己會不會。

Content: But like I'm doing a painting of it now.
Content_zh: 但我現在正在畫它。

Content: OK, man.
Content_zh: OK，老兄。

Content: I'm wondering about that Bagel filter thing.
Content_zh: 我在想那個 Bagel 濾鏡的事。

Content: I'm just wondering what it is, you know.
Content_zh: 我只是想知道那到底是什麼，你知道。

Content: What if Tim's happy with it.
Content_zh: 如果 Tim 滿意呢？

Content: I should talk to Tim.
Content_zh: 我應該跟 Tim 談談。

Content: Schafer: I have not talked to him about that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我還沒跟他談過那件事。

Content: I haven't talked to him on this trip about how he's feeling about everything.
Content_zh: 這趟他來，我還沒跟他談過他對整件事的感受。

Content: That'll be interesting.
Content_zh: 那應該會很有意思。

Content: Hopefully we get some alone time.
Content_zh: 希望我們能有點單獨相處的時間。

Content: Petty: I just don't know about the painting Bagel.
Content_zh: Petty：我只是不太確定 Bagel 繪畫這件事。

Content: Now how are you feeling about the like...
Content_zh: 那你現在對這個感覺怎麼樣……

Content: We've been going back and forth a long time just trying to get a feel for that whole process.
Content_zh: 我們來來回回很久了，只是想掌握整個流程的感覺。

Content: Stapley: The paining process? Petty: Yeah.
Content_zh: Stapley：繪畫流程？Petty：對。

Content: And then like work on this big ass fucking layered files and...
Content_zh: 然後還要處理這些超大的該死分層檔案，然後……

Content: Stapley: Yeah, yeah. I'm comfortable with that.
Content_zh: Stapley：對，對。那部分我沒問題。

Content: I'd like to actually get involved more with like the concept stuff, though.
Content_zh: 不過我其實想更參與概念方面的東西。

Content: Petty: Yeah?
Content_zh: Petty：是嗎？

Content: Stapley: I feel like I'm just kind of a painter.
Content_zh: Stapley：我覺得自己好像只是個畫工。

Content: I just feel like people are expecting it to be mine somehow, you know, just 'cause, you know.
Content_zh: 我只是覺得大家好像期待它某種程度上是我的東西，你知道，只因為，呃，你知道。

Content: That's what people are saying.
Content_zh: 大家都這樣說。

Content: And like it's supposed to be my style but it isn't really.
Content_zh: 而且它應該是我的風格，但其實不是。

Content: Petty: It may be that you're feeling like the painting isn't the side that you care about most.
Content_zh: Petty：也許你覺得繪畫不是你最在乎的那一面。

Content: Maybe you feel more about the shape, language and the concept design and we, you know, Levi does painting and you do layouts.
Content_zh: 也許你更在乎造型語言和概念設計，而我們可以，呃，Levi 做繪畫，你做配置。

Content: There's flexibility there.
Content_zh: 這中間有彈性。

Content: Stapley: Where like I don't paint everything but be kind of like overlook, you know.
Content_zh: Stapley：像是我不畫所有東西，而是比較像監督，你知道。

Content: Kind of just try to keep everything together.
Content_zh: 就是盡量把整體維持在一起。

Content: And that way I'm able to do some more idea stuff.
Content_zh: 那樣我就能做更多點子方面的事情。

Content: 'Cause even now the way it's going, like I don't think, I mean, like even this way
Content_zh: 因為就算照現在這樣走，我也不覺得，我是說，就算這樣，

Content: I don't think I'd be able to do all these paintings in four months.
Content_zh: 我也不覺得我能在四個月內完成所有這些畫。

Content: Seventy paintings by myself, anyway, you know.
Content_zh: 反正七十張畫，不可能我一個人全畫完。

Content: Well, at least if we're going to say like this is Bagel's style game, then I would, yeah, at least I would have a little bit more like yeah,
Content_zh: 嗯，至少如果我們要說這是 Bagel 風格的遊戲，那我至少會比較能覺得，

Content: you know, that is my stuff. You know.
Content_zh: 你知道，那是我的東西。

Content: Instead of like, ah, it's like an awesome Peter Chan drawing or something, you know, that I colored.
Content_zh: 而不是「啊，這是一張很棒的 Peter Chan 畫作之類的，只是我幫它上色」。

Content: Why don't we like finish off this sprint until the end of the month, right.
Content_zh: 不如我們先把這個 sprint 做到月底，對吧。

Content: I'll try to get all this cloud things going.
Content_zh: 我會試著把這些雲端的東西都推起來。

Content: Petty: But I think it's a tougher thing where that if we come up with a sketch of a plan that we think might work,
Content_zh: Petty：但我覺得比較難的是，如果我們想出一個覺得可能可行的粗略計畫，

Content: then I think we should try it sooner than later, 'cause fuck a sprint.
Content_zh: 那我覺得我們應該越早試越好，管他什麼 sprint。

Content: Who cares? I don't care.
Content_zh: 誰在乎？我不在乎。

Content: There's no publisher fucking us in the ass over it.
Content_zh: 又沒有發行商為了這件事把我們往死裡逼。

Content: Like with just the whole point is, it's just there to like be milestones as we make this game, right?
Content_zh: 整個重點只是，它就是在我們做遊戲時作為里程碑，對吧？

Content: So if we, you know, as long we're meeting Tim's goals for the project, as long as we think we can actually get it done
Content_zh: 所以只要我們能達成 Tim 對專案的目標，只要我們覺得真的做得完，

Content: and as long as we're all willing to give it a shot, there's no reason in the world we shouldn't be flexible. Right.
Content_zh: 也只要大家都願意試試看，就完全沒有理由不保持彈性。對吧。

Content: Stapley: I think it was because really when it first started, it was going to be like $300,000
Content_zh: Stapley：我想是因為一開始真的只是要做三十萬美元左右，

Content: and we're going to just try to do some retro Day of the Tentacle shit, you know.
Content_zh: 然後我們只是想做一些復古的《Day of the Tentacle》那種東西，你知道。

Content: And I know things change so much from the beginning of this Kickstarter thing, you know.
Content_zh: 我也知道從這個 Kickstarter 開始以來，事情已經變了很多，你知道。

Content: And I know now it's huge, it's a big thing, which is great.
Content_zh: 我也知道它現在很大，是個大東西，這很好。

Content: And it's awesome.
Content_zh: 而且很棒。

Content: But, I think we can work it out somehow.
Content_zh: 不過我覺得我們可以想辦法解決。

Content: Schafer: You know, right now Bagel's been doing a lot of painting, which is not necessarily, I can see where that would feel like,
Content_zh: Schafer：你知道，Bagel 現在一直在畫很多圖，雖然不一定，但我可以理解那種感覺，

Content: you're just like doing a coloring book or paint-by-numbers.
Content_zh: 你會覺得自己只是在做著色本，或照號碼上色。

Content: Those are just my terms, not his.
Content_zh: 那只是我的說法，不是他的。

Content: Stapley: I don't know.
Content_zh: Stapley：我不知道。

Content: I don't even know how it's going to turn out.
Content_zh: 我甚至不知道最後會變成怎樣。

Content: It might turn out totally different.
Content_zh: 也許結果會完全不同。

Content: I'm just kind of glad I got it off my chest, that's all.
Content_zh: 我只是有點高興自己把話說出來了，就這樣。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, I think his coming out here in person is what allowed him to open up about that.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我覺得他親自來這裡，才讓他能開口談那件事。

Content: I think he's over Skype.
Content_zh: 我想如果是透過 Skype，

Content: It's hard to really talk about, how you're feeling.
Content_zh: 要真的談自己的感受是很難的。

Content: Stapley: I'm glad I said it, because I was all worried about it.
Content_zh: Stapley：我很高興我說了，因為我一直很擔心。

Content: So I think so.
Content_zh: 所以我想是這樣。

Content: I think we're going to try to start doing that.
Content_zh: 我想我們會開始試著這麼做。

Content: Schafer: Like I'll feel a lot better once we made one of something.
Content_zh: Schafer：只要我們先做出一個像樣的東西，我就會安心很多。

Content: Like once, 'cause I was feeling like we had not yet accomplished, the game was looking good and coming along, right.
Content_zh: 因為我一直覺得我們還沒做到，遊戲看起來不錯，也有在往前，對吧。

Content: But we'd not yet accomplished that thing of seeing something in the playable version of the game that had that crazy Bagel-style yet.
Content_zh: 但我們還沒做到那件事，也就是在可玩版本裡看到帶有那種瘋狂 Bagel 風格的東西。

Content: Like the special element of like surrealism or just kind of dream logic that his paintings might have.
Content_zh: 像是他的畫裡可能有的那種超現實元素，或某種夢境邏輯。

Content: I hadn't yet seen in the game yet.
Content_zh: 我還沒在遊戲裡看到那個。

Content: So once we have something like that in the game, I'll be like, OK, great.
Content_zh: 所以一旦遊戲裡有那樣的東西，我就會覺得：「OK，很好。」

Content: We just need to do that a whole bunch of times then I'll feel a lot more relaxed.
Content_zh: 接著我們只要把那件事重複很多次，我就會放鬆很多。

Content: But until you achieve that, there's always this question of like will you be able to achieve what you want to do.
Content_zh: 但在你做到之前，心裡總會有個疑問：你到底能不能達成你想做的事。

Content: You know, it's tricky, 'cause you got to make, get the game to look the way you want it to look,
Content_zh: 你知道，這很棘手，因為你得讓遊戲看起來像你想要的樣子，

Content: but also get done on time with the people that you have and also all those people have to be happy.
Content_zh: 也要用現有的人手準時完成，而且所有人還都得開心。

Content: Yeah, like you're trying to get people to contribute their whole selfs to the project.
Content_zh: 對，就像你想讓大家把完整的自己投入這個專案。

Content: So that's challenging.
Content_zh: 所以那很有挑戰性。

Content: But it's what I spent the most of my time recently, like the last ten years recently, like doing, like trying to solve that problem.
Content_zh: 但那也是我最近大部分時間，或者說最近十年，大多都在做的事，試著解決這個問題。

Content: How to make a group of people work well together and bring the best ideas forth and make good stuff together.
Content_zh: 怎麼讓一群人好好合作，把最好的點子帶出來，一起做出好東西。

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
